commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Remaining action is maintainer/owner judgment on the new delegate API, lifecycle defaults, and merge-risk acceptance rather than a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue was found; the diff adds local TypeScript/Swift/docs/tests and does not add dependencies, workflow permissions, secret handling, or downloaded code execution. (sha=b5d4e017806d21eb21753f1d949cdf2e76f16479)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds hub-delegated persistent ACP sessions with delegate spawn/send/list/close flows, session metadata/protocol/config/docs updates, and regression coverage for label routing, lifecycle cleanup, metadata repair, and runtime handle reuse.

PR surface: Source +1669, Tests +2382, Docs +404, Config 0, Other +18. Total +4473 across 72 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a current-main bug report. Current main still rejects unthreaded ACP persistent sessions, while the PR supplies after-change screenshots for spawn, follow-up, close, and rejected post-close send.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Remaining action is maintainer/owner judgment on the new delegate API, lifecycle defaults, and merge-risk acceptance rather than a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Feat/acp hub delegated sessions
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem**: Hub sessions (WebChat, WeChat, and other main chat surfaces) need persistent external ACP harness workers without binding a Discord/Telegram thread. The existing ACP persistent path requires `mode: "session"` plus `thread: true`, which is unavailable or awkward on many hub channels. - **Why it matters now**: Dogfooding hub → Codex/Claude Code delegation from a single visible chat requires a named worker that stays alive across turns, accepts follow-ups by label, and does not echo A2A ping-pong back into the hub. - **Intended outcome**: A hub agent can `sessions_spawn({ runtime: "acp", delegate: true, label?, task })`, follow up with `sessions_send(label=...)`, list owned workers via `sessions_list({ delegated: true })`, and operators inspect or close workers with `/acp delegate list|status|close
target.author: scotthuang

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: scotthuang
